summary: Small fighter decomp matching fix for ftCo_80097D88 in ftCo_DownBound.c. The PR split the original function body into a new static helper named inlineA1, changed Fighter access from direct gobj->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), and made ftCo_80097D88 call the helper. Evidence indicates this was done specifically to fix matching for ftCo_80097D88.
what_changed: Renamed the previous ftCo_80097D88 implementation body into a new static helper function inlineA1.
Reintroduced ftCo_80097D88 as a wrapper that only calls inlineA1(gobj).
Changed local Fighter pointer initialization inside the helper from gobj->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Preserved the DownBound branch behavior: if fp->x2228_b2 is set, call ftCo_80097AF4(gobj); otherwise call ftCo_80097B24(gobj).
smart_moves: Used a wrapper/helper split to influence generated code while keeping the externally visible ftCo_80097D88 symbol present.
Used the project macro GET_FIGHTER(gobj) instead of direct user_data access, which likely better matches existing decomp idioms and generated code expectations.
Kept the logic change minimal: the behavioral branch stayed identical, limiting risk while addressing matching.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn matching issues, wrapping the function body in a static helper can alter inlining/call structure without changing runtime behavior.

Direct field access such as gobj->user_data and helper macros such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj) may compile differently or better express intended project conventions; try the macro form when matching fighter code.
Preserving the named target symbol as a thin wrapper can satisfy symbol boundaries while allowing compiler shaping through a local static helper.
A very small source restructure can be enough to fix matching when the control flow is already semantically correct.
naming_conventions: Target function retained the existing unresolved-style name ftCo_80097D88.
Temporary/static helper was named inlineA1, following a matching-oriented helper naming pattern rather than a semantic gameplay name.
Fighter object parameter uses the common Fighter_GObj* gobj convention.
Fighter pointer local uses the common fp convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a static helper to encourage or model an inline/call boundary needed for matching.
Converted ftCo_80097D88 into a one-call wrapper around inlineA1.
Replaced direct gobj->user_data load with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), likely to align with expected macro expansion or project-wide fighter access style.
No assembly excerpt or explicit compiler explanation was provided, so the exact codegen reason is inferred only from the diff and PR title.
